creator: stas@FreeBSD.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

On Tue, 21 Apr 2009 18:20:02 GMT
Tijl Coosemans <tijl@ulyssis.org> mentioned:

> The following reply was made to PR ports/133856; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Tijl Coosemans <tijl@ulyssis.org>
> To: bug-followup@freebsd.org, swhetzel@gmail.com
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/133856: graphics/lcms: build of kde-koffice3 fails with lcms-1.18,1
> Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2009 20:15:22 +0200
> 
>  I ran into this when updating digikam. Installing .la files is
>  generally accepted nowadays, so I hope Scot's patch can be committed.
>  While rebuilding all lcms dependent ports would probably work it's
>  something that should be avoided if possible. It's way too much work
>  compared to installing a tiny 800 byte .la file.
> 

That's exactly the reason why .la files should not be installed. In fact
installing .la on FreeBSD could be considered a bug.

The problem you're referring to would not happen If I bumped revisons
of all affected ports that I forgot. For now, committing this patch
is no-point as it'll require the yet one rebuilding of all ports.

- -- 
Stanislav Sedov
ST4096-RIPE
